root.help:
usage: fslc [-h] [-V] {version,check,lint,migrate,fmt,kernel,conformance,verify,sweep,scenarios,replay,testgen,mutate,explain,html,ledger,document,approval,refine,diff,chain,typestate,analyze,db,compat,ai,domain}

positional arguments:
  {version,check,lint,migrate,fmt,kernel,conformance,verify,sweep,scenarios,replay,testgen,mutate,explain,html,ledger,document,approval,refine,diff,chain,typestate,analyze,db,compat,ai,domain}
    version             show the version
    lint                report edition migration diagnostics
    migrate             plan or atomically apply edition migrations
    fmt                 format one FSL source or check multiple paths
    kernel              emit the normalized public Kernel JSON contract
    conformance         emit language-neutral concrete conformance vectors
    sweep               run bounded verification across a scope grid
    html                generate a self-contained HTML review report
    ledger              generate a business audit ledger (markdown) by
                        requirement id
    document            generate a requirements document (ja/en) or emit its
                        RCIR claim set as JSON
    approval            create and check digest-bound approval records
    diff                compare OLD and NEW specification semantics
    chain               run a project manifest across business, requirements,
                        design, and impl
    typestate           decide whether typestate (ghost types) applies to a
                        design spec and emit a TS template
    analyze             emit structural analysis JSON for a spec
    db                  database compatibility dialect commands
    compat              shared compatibility commands
    ai                  AI dialect commands
    domain              Functional DDD / async effect dialect commands

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -V, --version         show program's version number and exit

commands.help:
usage: fslc ai [-h] {check,replay,eval,regress,compare,drift,compat}

positional arguments:
  {check,replay,eval,regress,compare,drift,compat}
    check               check an ai_component hard contract or recursive agent
                        structure
    replay              compare AI runtime JSONL events to ai_component
                        declarations
    eval                evaluate precomputed AI eval JSONL against
                        statistical_property thresholds
    regress             check ai_migration no_regression metrics
    compare             compare two precomputed AI eval JSONL files
    drift               check observed_property thresholds and drift from
                        runtime telemetry
    compat              generate an AI artifact capability profile for dbsystem

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

usage: fslc ai check [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--engine {bmc,induction}] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --engine {bmc,induction}
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}

usage: fslc ai compare [-h] --from FROM_RECORDS --to TO_RECORDS [--from-label FROM_LABEL] [--to-label TO_LABEL] [--dataset DATASET]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --from FROM_RECORDS
  --to TO_RECORDS
  --from-label FROM_LABEL
  --to-label TO_LABEL
  --dataset DATASET

usage: fslc ai compat [-h] [--environment ENVIRONMENT] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --environment ENVIRONMENT

usage: fslc ai drift [-h] --logs LOGS [--baseline-logs BASELINE_LOGS] [--window WINDOW] [--baseline BASELINE] [--property PROPERTY_NAME] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --logs LOGS
  --baseline-logs BASELINE_LOGS
  --window WINDOW
  --baseline BASELINE
  --property PROPERTY_NAME

usage: fslc ai eval [-h] [--records RECORDS] [--dataset DATASET] [--slice SLICE_NAME] [--property PROPERTY_NAME] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --records RECORDS
  --dataset DATASET
  --slice SLICE_NAME
  --property PROPERTY_NAME

usage: fslc ai regress [-h] [--migration MIGRATION] --before-records BEFORE_RECORDS --after-records AFTER_RECORDS [--dataset DATASET] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --migration MIGRATION
  --before-records BEFORE_RECORDS
  --after-records AFTER_RECORDS
  --dataset DATASET

usage: fslc ai replay [-h] --logs LOGS [--component COMPONENT] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --logs LOGS
  --component COMPONENT
                        ai_component to replay when FILE is a project-level fsl-
                        ai file

usage: fslc analyze [-h] [--projection {tsg,action_dependency_graph,action_state_graph,code_audit,impact_graph,property_state_graph,requirement_property_graph,refinement_graph,traceability_graph}] [--profile {ai-review}] [--export {tag-review}] [--code CODE] [--focus FOCUS] [--format {dot,json,mermaid}] file [file ...]

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --projection {tsg,action_dependency_graph,action_state_graph,code_audit,impact_graph,property_state_graph,requirement_property_graph,refinement_graph,traceability_graph}
                        structural projection to emit
  --profile {ai-review}
                        emit AI-readable structural review findings
  --export {tag-review}
                        export declaration-level tag/formula review tuples
  --code CODE           source file or directory for --projection code_audit
  --focus FOCUS         node id for --projection impact_graph, e.g. state:stock
                        or action:checkout
  --format {dot,json,mermaid}

usage: fslc chain [-h] [--keep-going] [path]

positional arguments:
  path

options:
  -h, --help    show this help message and exit
  --keep-going

usage: fslc check [-h] [--strict-tags] [--requirements REQUIREMENTS] [--edition {current,next}] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --strict-tags
  --requirements REQUIREMENTS
  --edition {current,next}

usage: fslc compat [-h] {check}

positional arguments:
  {check}
    check     check dbsystem compatibility, optionally including AI capability
              profiles

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit

usage: fslc compat check [-h] [--include-ai] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help    show this help message and exit
  --include-ai

usage: fslc db [-h] {check,observe,import}

positional arguments:
  {check,observe,import}
    check               check a dbsystem and emit fsl-db findings
    observe             compare runtime observation logs to dbsystem
                        declarations
    import              import SQL DDL or a minimal ORM schema into dbsystem

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

usage: fslc db check [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--engine {bmc,induction}] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --engine {bmc,induction}
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}

usage: fslc db import [-h] [--name NAME] [--source {auto,sql,prisma}] [-o OUTPUT] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --name NAME
  --source {auto,sql,prisma}
                        source format to import (default: auto by extension)
  -o, --output OUTPUT

usage: fslc db observe [-h] --trace TRACE file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help     show this help message and exit
  --trace TRACE

usage: fslc diff [-h] [--git BASE..HEAD] [--depth DEPTH] [--mapping MAPPING] [--forbid FORBID] [old] [new]

positional arguments:
  old
  new

options:
  -h, --help         show this help message and exit
  --git BASE..HEAD   compare a spec (or every changed .fsl file) across two Git
                     revisions
  --depth DEPTH
  --mapping MAPPING  optional refinement mapping for one comparison direction
  --forbid FORBID    comma-separated finding kinds that should fail the gate

usage: fslc domain [-h] {check,analyze,expand,generate,replay,testgen}

positional arguments:
  {check,analyze,expand,generate,replay,testgen}
    check               check domain/effect structure and verify generated
                        kernel
    analyze             emit aggregate/effect ownership findings
    expand              expand domain/effect dialect to kernel FSL
    generate            generate Functional DDD implementation scaffold
    replay              replay runtime command/event/effect logs
    testgen             generate domain adapter/conformance scaffold

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

usage: fslc domain analyze [-h] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit

usage: fslc domain expand [-h] [-o OUTPUT] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help           show this help message and exit
  -o, --output OUTPUT

usage: fslc domain generate [-h] [--profile {functional-ddd}] [--target {typescript,kotlin,swift,python,rust}] [-o OUTPUT] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --profile {functional-ddd}
  --target {typescript,kotlin,swift,python,rust}
  -o, --output OUTPUT

usage: fslc domain replay [-h] --logs LOGS file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help   show this help message and exit
  --logs LOGS

usage: fslc domain testgen [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--target {vitest,pytest,swift,kotlin,dart,phpunit}] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] [--strict] [-o OUTPUT] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --target {vitest,pytest,swift,kotlin,dart,phpunit}
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --strict
  -o, --output OUTPUT

usage: fslc explain [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--readable] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help     show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --readable

usage: fslc html [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [-o OUTPUT] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] [--engine {bmc,induction}] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  -o, --output OUTPUT
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --engine {bmc,induction}
                        use k-induction so a proved report can show assurance
                        class 'proved'

usage: fslc approval [-h] {create,check,diff}

positional arguments:
  {create,check,diff}
    create              bind a reviewed artifact to the normalized spec digest
    check               compare a spec and regenerated artifact to an approval record
    diff                run semantic diff from the approved Git baseline

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

usage: fslc approval create [-h] --kind {ledger,html,scenarios} --artifact ARTIFACT --approver APPROVER [--requirement ID] [--signing-key PRIVATE_KEY] [--depth DEPTH] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] [--engine {bmc,induction}] [-o OUTPUT] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --kind {ledger,html,scenarios}
  --artifact ARTIFACT   reviewed artifact generated with the recorded inputs
  --approver APPROVER
  --requirement ID      requirement ID to approve; repeatable, defaults to all
  --signing-key PRIVATE_KEY
                        create a signed v2 record with a PKCS#8 Ed25519 key
  --depth DEPTH
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --engine {bmc,induction}
  -o, --output OUTPUT

usage: fslc approval check [-h] --record RECORD [--trust-key PUBLIC_KEY] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --record RECORD
  --trust-key PUBLIC_KEY
                        trusted SPKI Ed25519 public key; repeatable

usage: fslc approval diff [-h] --record RECORD [--depth DEPTH] [--trust-key PUBLIC_KEY] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --record RECORD
  --depth DEPTH
  --trust-key PUBLIC_KEY
                        trusted SPKI Ed25519 public key; repeatable

usage: fslc ledger [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [-o OUTPUT] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] [--impl-log IMPL_LOG] [--engine {bmc,induction}] [--evidence EVIDENCE] [--approval RECORD] [--trust-key PUBLIC_KEY] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  -o, --output OUTPUT
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --impl-log IMPL_LOG   optional implementation trace JSON to score conformance
                        (fslc replay)
  --engine {bmc,induction}
                        use k-induction so a requirement's assurance class can
                        reach 'proved' (#171)
  --evidence EVIDENCE   path to a saved stdout envelope (JSON) from an external-
                        evidence producer (fslc ai eval/replay/drift, fslc db
                        observe, fslc domain replay, ...); repeatable
  --approval RECORD     digest-bound approval record to check; repeatable
  --trust-key PUBLIC_KEY
                        trusted SPKI Ed25519 public key; repeatable

usage: fslc document [-h] {generate,claims,check}

positional arguments:
  {generate,claims,check}
    generate            render a controlled-language requirements document
                        (ja/en) from the checked spec
    claims              emit the Requirement Claim IR (RCIR) claim set as
                        JSON
    check               detect drift between a generated document and a
                        fresh re-render of its spec

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

usage: fslc document generate [-h] [--view {requirements}] [--lang {ja,en}] [--strict] [--strict-rendering] [--glossary GLOSSARY] [--evidence EVIDENCE] [-o OUTPUT] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --view {requirements}
                        v1 supports only requirements; reserved for
                        business/design (#334)
  --lang {ja,en}        controlled-language locale (default: ja)
  --strict              fail if an authored target is unattributed or
                        unsupported (FSL-DOC-UNTAGGED-TARGET /
                        FSL-DOC-UNSUPPORTED-TARGET), or a glossary target
                        does not exist (FSL-DOC-LABEL-UNKNOWN)
  --strict-rendering    fail if an expression falls back to canonical FSL
                        text (FSL-DOC-FORMULA-FALLBACK)
  --glossary GLOSSARY   presentation-only display-label sidecar
                        (fslc.document-glossary.v1); a duplicate target is
                        always an error (FSL-DOC-LABEL-CONFLICT)
  --evidence EVIDENCE   saved evidence envelope (JSON, same shape as 'fslc
                        ledger --evidence') overlaid onto the document as a
                        per-requirement assurance class
                        (proved/bounded/replay-observed/statistical); does
                        not run any verification itself; repeatable; an
                        unmatched file is a warning by default, an error
                        under --strict (FSL-DOC-EVIDENCE-UNMATCHED)
  -o, --output OUTPUT

usage: fslc document claims [-h] [--view {requirements}] [-o OUTPUT] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --view {requirements}
                        v1 supports only requirements; reserved for
                        business/design (#334)
  -o, --output OUTPUT

usage: fslc document check [-h] [--glossary GLOSSARY] [--evidence EVIDENCE] file document

positional arguments:
  file      the FSL spec the document was generated from
  document  the generated Markdown artifact (from 'fslc document generate')

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --glossary GLOSSARY   the same glossary sidecar 'generate' was given, if
                        any; a mismatch (missing, extra, or different
                        content) is reported as glossary_changed
                        (FSL-DOC-GLOSSARY-CHANGED), not a hard error
  --evidence EVIDENCE   the same evidence files 'generate' was given, if
                        any; a mismatch is reported as evidence_changed
                        (FSL-DOC-EVIDENCE-CHANGED), not a hard error;
                        repeatable

Structural drift check only: no natural language is interpreted. Reads the
locale and source label back from the artifact's own frontmatter, re-projects
and re-renders the spec, and compares claim/slot marker structure and
claim-block digests. Exit codes: 0 document_conformant, 1 document_drifted,
2 parse/type/semantics/schema/I/O error, 3 internal error.

usage: fslc mutate [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--by-requirement] [--max-mutants MAX_MUTANTS] [--from MUTANTS_FROM] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --by-requirement
  --max-mutants MAX_MUTANTS
  --from MUTANTS_FROM   adjudicate external JSONL mutants in addition to the
                        built-in catalog

usage: fslc refine [-h] [--depth DEPTH] impl abs mapping [rest ...]

positional arguments:
  impl
  abs
  mapping
  rest           chain check: appending more (abs map) pairs runs an end-to-end
                 composed check

options:
  -h, --help     show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH

usage: fslc replay [-h] (--trace TRACE | --from-log FROM_LOG) [--mapping MAPPING] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help           show this help message and exit
  --trace TRACE
  --from-log FROM_LOG  production JSONL records to map and replay
  --mapping MAPPING    refinement-syntax log mapping (required with --from-log)

usage: fslc scenarios [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}

usage: fslc sweep [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--engine {bmc,induction}] [--k K_IND] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] [--vacuity {warn,error,ignore}] [--property PROPERTY_NAME] [--instances INSTANCES] [--values VALUES] [--strict-tags] [--requirements REQUIREMENTS] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH         depth range LO..HI to sweep (default: 0..8)
  --engine {bmc,induction}
  --k K_IND             max induction depth (induction engine only)
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --vacuity {warn,error,ignore}
  --property PROPERTY_NAME
                        check a single named property in isolation
  --instances INSTANCES
                        sweep an entity bound (NAME=LO..HI; repeatable)
  --values VALUES       sweep a number upper bound as LO..LO through LO..HI
  --strict-tags
  --requirements REQUIREMENTS

usage: fslc testgen [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [-o OUTPUT] [--target {pytest,vitest,swift,kotlin,dart,phpunit}] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] [--strict] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  -o, --output OUTPUT
  --target {pytest,vitest,swift,kotlin,dart,phpunit}
                        test harness to emit (default: pytest)
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --strict

usage: fslc typestate [-h] [--ts] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
  --ts        emit only the derivable entities' TypeScript to stdout instead of
              JSON

usage: fslc verify [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--engine {bmc,induction,explicit,auto}] [--k K_IND] [--explicit-budget EXPLICIT_BUDGET] [--deadlock {warn,error,ignore}] [--vacuity {warn,error,ignore}] [--property PROPERTY_NAME] [--exclude-property EXCLUDE_PROPERTY_NAMES] [--instances INSTANCES] [--values VALUES] [--strict-tags] [--requirements REQUIREMENTS] [--no-cache] [--lemma LEMMAS] [--from-state FROM_STATE] [--edition {current,next}] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH
  --engine {bmc,induction,explicit,auto}
  --k K_IND             max induction depth (induction engine only)
  --explicit-budget EXPLICIT_BUDGET
                        max visited states for the explicit engine (default
                        1000000); exceeding returns unknown_budget
  --deadlock {warn,error,ignore}
  --vacuity {warn,error,ignore}
  --property PROPERTY_NAME
                        check a single named property in isolation; resolves
                        across invariant/trans/leadsTo/reachable declarations
  --exclude-property EXCLUDE_PROPERTY_NAMES
                        skip a named property; repeat to omit
                        invariants/trans/leadsTo/reachable declarations
  --instances INSTANCES
                        override a verify-block 'instances NAME = N' bound
                        (NAME=N; repeatable) — e.g. shrink to 1 entity for
                        liveness
  --values VALUES       override a verify-block 'values NAME = LO..HI' bound
                        (NAME=LO..HI; repeatable)
  --strict-tags
  --requirements REQUIREMENTS
  --no-cache            skip the persistent verdict cache for this run (neither
                        reads nor writes it)
  --lemma LEMMAS        candidate auxiliary invariant expression (induction
                        only; repeatable)
  --from-state FROM_STATE
                        replace spec init with a complete Monitor/replay
                        logical-state JSON snapshot (BMC only)
  --edition {current,next}

usage: fslc conformance [-h] [--depth DEPTH] [--kernel-version {1,2}] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --depth DEPTH         maximum transition depth (default: 4)
  --kernel-version {1,2}
                        public Kernel major (default: 1)

usage: fslc kernel [-h] [--kernel-version {1,2}] file

positional arguments:
  file

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --kernel-version {1,2}
                        public Kernel major (default: 1)

usage: fslc fmt [-h] [--check] [--edition {current,next}] path [path ...]

Format one registered FSL document to stdout without mutating it. Multiple paths are accepted only with --check. Use '-' for stdin.

positional arguments:
  path

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --check               report whether formatting is required (exit 0 clean, 1 changed)
  --edition {current,next}
                        formatting policy (default: current)

usage: fslc lint [-h] [--edition {current,next}] [--project PROJECT] path [path ...]

Report stable edition and ID-policy diagnostics without mutating source files.

positional arguments:
  path

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --edition {current,next}
                        lint policy (default: current)
  --project PROJECT     explicit fsl-project.toml ID-policy manifest

usage: fslc migrate [-h] [--edition {current,next}] [--write] path [path ...]

Plan machine-applicable edition edits without mutation. --write applies the validated file set atomically.

positional arguments:
  path

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --edition {current,next}
                        target edition (default: next)
  --write               atomically replace every validated input file

usage: fslc version [-h]

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
